name: Alti
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometrical sans serif with modernist and art deco influence; tall proportions and clean geometric construction place it firmly in the geometric sans category.
summary: A tall, single-weight geometric sans with art deco and modernist references, released in 2022 by Good Type Foundry.
description: Built on geometric principles with tall proportions, Alti draws from modernism and art deco to produce a distinctive single-weight sans. It ships in one Regular style with alternates, oldstyle figures and European character support across a broad Latin Extended glyph set. The narrow, upright character makes it best suited to display and headline settings where its architectural geometry can carry the work.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Alti as a tall geometrical sans serif with inspiration from modernism and art deco, available in one versatile Regular weight with alternates, European language support and oldstyle figures, released in 2022.

name: Agentur
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a calligraphic sans serif; its contemporary features and pen-influenced forms place it firmly in the calligraphic sans subcategory rather than humanist or geometric.
summary: A calligraphic sans with contemporary detailing, offered in Regular and Display styles with broad European language support.
description: Released in 2016, this single-weight family draws on calligraphic letter construction while keeping its finish contemporary and clean. The two styles, Regular and Display, serve different size contexts, and the character set covers 172 accented glyphs for wide European language support alongside ligatures, fractions and a full complement of symbols. It suits editorial and identity work where a touch of pen-influenced warmth is wanted without resorting to an overtly script-style face.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Agentur as a calligraphic sans serif typeface with contemporary features, available in two styles, Regular and Display, with one weight, European language support and ligatures, released in 2016.

name: Adieu
classification_notes: A wide, extended sans serif designed primarily for display and heading use, with proportions and forms inspired by high speed. Its extended width and four-weight range place it firmly in the display grotesque subcategory.
summary: An extended display sans with speed-influenced proportions, released in four weights across twelve styles with European Latin and basic Cyrillic coverage.
description: Designed for headings, logos and display work, Adieu draws its formal energy from the aesthetics of high speed, resulting in wide, confident letterforms. Four weights run across twelve styles, with alternates included for additional flexibility. Cyrillic support is available in beta alongside a broad European Latin character set.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Adieu as an extended sans serif typeface inspired by high speed, developed primarily for headings, logos and display work, though they note it has also been used effectively as chunky text setting. Released in 2017, it offers four weights in twelve styles with alternates and covers European Latin alongside basic Cyrillic in beta.

name: Chapter
classification_notes: Described as aggressive with extended sharp serifs and distinct stroke contrast, placing it firmly in the display serif category where dramatic, high-contrast letterforms are the primary intent.
summary: A high-contrast display serif with sharp, extended serifs across three weights, released in 2017.
description: A display serif built around aggressive contrast and elongated sharp serifs that give it an edgy, editorial character. Available in Light, Regular and Bold, the family covers European language support and includes ligatures, making it usable across a range of Latin-based typesetting. A compact but purposeful release suited to headlines and branding work that needs weight and edge.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Chapter as an aggressive serif typeface featuring extended sharp serifs and distinct contrasts, available in three weights — Light, Regular and Bold — with European language support and ligatures, released in 2017.

name: Byrd
classification_notes: A rounded sans serif with chunky proportions and a retro character, particularly in heavier weights. The rounded terminals place it firmly in the rounded sans subcategory.
summary: A chunky rounded sans in seven weights with italics, carrying a retro warmth that becomes more pronounced in heavier cuts.
description: Released in 2020 by Good Type Foundry, Byrd is a rounded sans serif spanning seven weights and fourteen styles including matching italics. The design has a cheerful, slightly retro character that grows more expressive at heavier weights, making it well suited to display use, branding and packaging where a friendly, rounded voice is needed. Glyph coverage includes 172 accented characters for broad European language support.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Byrd as chunky, happy and a bit retro, particularly in its heavier weights. It comes in seven weights with corresponding italics and supports European languages.

name: Ekstra
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a neo grotesque, with a notably tall x-height and clean, rational letterforms typical of the neo-grotesque genre.
summary: A neo-grotesque with an unusually tall x-height, offered in five weights with matching italics and broad European language support.
description: Released in 2019, this neo-grotesque distinguishes itself chiefly through its extra tall x-height, which lends text a distinctive density and legibility at small sizes. Ten styles run across five weights from Light to Bold, each paired with an italic. European language support covers an extensive range of accented Latin characters.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Ekstra as a contemporary neo grotesque with an extra tall x-height, available in five weights from Light to Bold with corresponding italics, released in 2019 with European language support.

name: Figue
classification_notes: A high-contrast, slightly condensed serif with rounded serifs and tall x-height, characteristics that place it firmly in the display serif category, designed for headline and decorative use rather than extended text.
summary: A botanical-inspired display serif with rounded serifs, tall x-height and high contrast, released in a single weight.
description: Named after the ficus plant family, this slightly condensed serif draws on organic references while maintaining crisp, high-contrast letterforms suited to editorial headlines and packaging. The rounded serifs give it a softness that tempers the contrast, making it feel refined rather than severe. Released in 2018 with European language support and oldstyle figures, it covers a broad Latin Extended character set across a single Regular style.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Figue as a botanical-inspired serif named after the ficus plant family. It is slightly condensed with a tall x-height, rounded serifs and high contrast, available in one weight with European language support and oldstyle figures, released in 2018.

name: Faxi
classification_notes: Faxi is an uppercase-only display typeface with a strong decorative character, originally commissioned for an interior design firm, suggesting expressive display use rather than text setting.
summary: A single-weight display typeface originally commissioned for Stockholm interior firm Maldini Studios, refined and released for licensing in 2020.
description: Designed by Jens Nilsson and Kenneth Knutsen at Good Type Foundry, Faxi began life as a bespoke typeface called Donadoni for Maldini Studios in Stockholm. The family consists of one weight and one style, with broad European language support across its uppercase, lowercase, accented and symbol character sets. Its origins in interior branding give it a measured, considered character suited to editorial and identity work where restraint is a virtue.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Faxi as a typeface designed by Jens Nilsson and Kenneth Knutsen, originally named Donadoni and created for Stockholm-based interior firm Maldini Studios. Refined throughout 2020 and released as a limited edition, it is available for licensing as a single weight with European language support.

name: Good Sans
classification_notes: The foundry describes Good Sans as a contemporary sans serif inspired by mid-century neo-grotesques, placing it squarely in that tradition alongside typefaces like Helvetica and Univers.
summary: A neo-grotesque sans in six weights with italics and geometric alternates, released in 2018 and inspired by mid-century Swiss-style grotesques.
description: Good Sans draws on the formal language of mid-century neo-grotesques, offering six weights from Thin to Black with corresponding italics. A geometric alternate character set and stylistic set extend its range beyond the core neo-grotesque palette. European language support is included via a broad accented Latin glyph set.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Good Sans as a contemporary sans serif typeface inspired by mid-century neo-grotesques, available in six weights from Thin to Black with matching italics, geometric alternate characters, a stylistic set and European language support. Released in 2018.

name: Good Mono
classification_notes: A monospaced companion to Good Sans, itself a neo-grotesque; the fixed-width format and grotesque forms place it firmly in the monospaced grotesque subcategory.
summary: A monospaced companion to Good Sans, drawing on mid-century neo-grotesque forms and released in a single weight.
description: Designed as the fixed-width counterpart to Good Sans, Good Mono applies the proportions and spirit of mid-century neo-grotesques to a monospaced grid. It ships in one weight with broad European language support, covering accented Latin characters across 172 glyphs. The restrained single-style offering suits coding environments, data display or editorial contexts where a clean, unfussy mono is needed.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Good Mono as the monospaced counterpart to Good Sans, a contemporary sans serif inspired by mid-century neo-grotesques. Released in 2020, it comes in one weight and one style with European language support.

name: Kosmos
classification_notes: Kosmos is built around overlapping, expanding shapes that intentionally dissolve into illegibility at heavier weights, placing it firmly in experimental display territory with no conventional classification equivalent.
summary: An experimental display system of 10 weights built on overlapping shapes that progress from faint and subtle to a deliberately unreadable mass.
description: Kosmos treats weight not as a measure of stroke thickness but as a degree of overlap, starting with a light, ghostly presence and accumulating into dense, illegible muddiness at its heaviest. The ten styles are named Misty and Boggy at the lighter end, suggesting atmosphere over utility. Coverage is limited to European Latin with uppercase, lowercase, accented glyphs and standard punctuation.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Kosmos as an experimental type system with expandable overlapping shapes, released in 2022. The ten weights range from a subtle, understated presence through to total unreadability, with European language support across 10 styles.

name: Kubik
classification_notes: A semi-monospaced, all-caps modular display face with a grid-based construction typical of 1980s digital and synth-culture aesthetics, placing it firmly in the display category.
summary: A semi-monospaced, all-caps modular display face drawn from 1980s synth music and visual culture, released in a single Black weight.
description: Built on a modular grid with semi-monospaced spacing, Kubik is an all-uppercase display font with roots in the digital aesthetics of 1980s synth culture. The lowercase slot offers an alternate, sharper cut of select characters rather than a true lowercase alphabet. Despite a single weight and style, it ships with broad European language support across 172 accented glyphs.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Kubik as a semi-monospaced modular display font inspired by the synth music and visual culture of the 1980s. It is all uppercase, with the lowercase position providing a sharper alternate version of certain characters. Released in 2019 in one weight and style, it includes European language support.

name: Mycena
classification_notes: Mycena is explicitly described as graffiti-inspired and is upper case only in a single Black weight, placing it firmly in the display category with a graffiti subcategory.
summary: A graffiti-inspired fat cap display face released in a single Black weight, uppercase only, with alternate glyphs mapped to lowercase.
description: Drawn from graffiti lettering traditions, Mycena offers a fat cap Black weight suited to posters, logotypes and headline work where bold personality is needed. The character set is uppercase only, with 26 alternate glyphs accessible via the lowercase slots and broad European accent coverage across 172 accented characters. A single-style release with a clear scope and no pretension to versatility.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Mycena as a graffiti-inspired display font with a mushroomy name, available in one weight — Black — and uppercase only, with some alternates accessible as lowercase glyphs. Released in 2023 with European language support.

name: Okkult
classification_notes: A single-weight display typeface rooted in 1970s horror film and doom/stoner visual culture, making it firmly a display face with no body-text application intended.
summary: A single-weight display typeface drawn from 1970s horror film and early doom and stoner visual culture, released by Good Type Foundry in 2022.
description: Rooted in the aesthetic language of seventies horror cinema and doom metal typography, this face offers one weight in a distinctly dark, occult-inflected style. Coverage extends to European Latin with 172 accented glyphs, making it serviceable across most Western European languages. The single Black weight limits it to headlines, posters and short display settings where the atmosphere is the point.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Okkult as having its roots in the seventies, specifically in horror film and early doom and stoner visuals. Released in 2022, it is offered in one weight and one style with European Latin support.

name: Motti
designer: Mathis El Idrissi and Kenneth Knutsen
classification_notes: Explicitly described as a monospaced typeface with spiky serifs, making it a monospaced serif. The serifs are described as spiky, giving it a distinctive display character across its weight range.
summary: A seven-weight monospaced serif with pointed, spiky serifs that scale from refined text use to bold display work.
description: Designed by Mathis El Idrissi and Kenneth Knutsen for Good Type Foundry, Motti pairs a strict monospaced grid with angular, spiky serifs across seven weights from Thin to Black. The lighter weights carry a sharp elegance suited to running text and editorial settings, while the heavier cuts become expressive enough for titles and display use. European language support is included via an extended Latin glyph set.
foundry_blurb: GTF describe Motti as a monospaced typeface with spiky serifs, offering seven weights and a single upright style. They note its range from thin weights that bring elegance and refinement to a Black weight suitable as a striking display font for titles, positioning it as a versatile release with broad application.

name: Ridder
classification_notes: Ridder draws from Roman and Greek stone carving traditions, producing high-contrast serifs with an experimental approach to letterform construction and overlap, placing it firmly in display serif territory.
summary: A high-contrast display serif drawn from Roman and Greek stone-carving traditions, available in seven weights as a variable font.
description: GTF Ridder takes its forms from inscriptional lettering, referencing the clarity and restraint of letters cut into stone rather than set in metal. Seven weights run from Light to Black, offered as a variable font with European language support. The foundry describes an abstract approach to legibility, with letterforms that push shape, overlap and contrast beyond conventional bounds.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Ridder as a typeface shaped by stone and time, loosely inspired by Roman and Greek carving traditions. They cite clarity, strength and quiet authority as its guiding qualities, with an abstract approach to legibility that pushes shapes, overlaps and contrast into new territory. Seven weights from Light to Black are available, including variable font support and European language coverage, scheduled for release in 2026.

name: Plakat Grotesk
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a grotesque typeface, with visual inspiration drawn from old posters, street signs and newspapers — placing it firmly in the display grotesque tradition.
summary: A two-weight display grotesque drawn from old European posters, street signage and newspaper type, with a distinctly quirky character.
description: Originally drawn in 2014 and released commercially in 2016, Plakat Grotesk takes its cues from the rough, idiosyncratic grotesques found on period posters and street signs. It ships in two weights with two styles and covers a broad range of European languages through an extended Latin glyph set of over 300 characters. The quirkiness of its letterforms makes it better suited to headline and display use than running text.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Plakat as a quirky grotesque typeface inspired by typefaces found on old posters, street signs and newspapers, originally designed in 2014 and released in 2016 with two weights, two styles and European language support.

name: Opposit
classification_notes: Classified as sans-serif with a high contrast subcategory due to its defining feature of reversed or opposite stroke contrast compared to conventional typefaces, placing it in a display-oriented category distinct from standard grotesque or humanist sans families.
summary: A high-contrast sans with reversed stroke stress, running across eight weights with European language support.
description: Released in 2016, Opposit inverts the conventional relationship between thick and thin strokes, giving it a distinctly unconventional rhythm suited to display and editorial work. Eight weights from Light to Heavy each come with a matching style, and the family includes stylistic sets and alternates for additional flexibility. European language support covers an extended Latin glyph set of over 300 characters.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Opposit as a high contrast sans serif with opposite contrasts from your average font, ranging from Light to Heavy across eight weights and eight styles, with European language support and stylistic alternates.

name: Riposte
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a neo grotesque inspired by mid-century fonts with contemporary features, placing it firmly in the neo-grotesque sans-serif tradition.
summary: A neo-grotesque sans with mid-century roots, offered in ten weights with matching italics added in 2022.
description: First released in 2016 with just regular and bold cuts, Riposte was expanded in 2017 to cover ten weights across 20 styles, with italics following in 2022. It draws on mid-century grotesque conventions while incorporating contemporary detailing, and ships with broad European language support across an extensive accented character set. A workmanlike family suited to editorial and identity work that wants a clean, neutral grotesque base.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Riposte as a neo grotesque typeface inspired by mid-century fonts, mixed with contemporary features. Originally released in 2016 in regular and bold, it was re-released in 2017 with extra features, multiple weights and styles, with italics added in 2022, totalling 10 weights and 20 styles with European language support.

name: Solina
classification_notes: Described explicitly as grotesque, spanning thin to heavy across condensed to extended widths, consistent with a contemporary neo-grotesque superfamily.
summary: A large grotesque superfamily spanning ten weights and three widths, with italics throughout, totalling 60 styles.
description: Released in 2021 by Good Type Foundry, Solina covers a wide range of typographic needs across condensed, regular and extended widths, each in ten weights from thin to heavy with supporting italics. The 60-style family includes European language support with an extensive accented character set of 172 glyphs. A functional workhorse intended to handle varied typographic demands without switching families.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Solina as a modern, massive grotesque superfamily running from thin to heavy and condensed to extended, with supporting italics across all widths. The 60-font family is designed to cover all typographic needs and includes European language support.

name: Rouleur
classification_notes: A single-weight black display sans with a tall x-height and extended proportions, clearly intended for large-scale logo and packaging use rather than text setting.
summary: A single-weight black display sans drawn from cycling culture, with a tall x-height suited to logos and packaging.
description: Released in 2019, this black-only sans draws its personality from cycling aesthetics, pairing chunky strokes with extended character proportions. The tall x-height gives it strong legibility at display sizes, making it well suited to logo lockups, product packaging and short-burst branding. Coverage spans European Latin with 172 accented glyphs, though the family offers no weight or style variation beyond its single Black cut.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Rouleur as a chunky yet sophisticated sans serif inspired by cycling culture, noting its tall x-height and extended characters as features suited to logos, branding and packaging design. Released in 2019, it comes in a single Black weight with European language support.

name: Soya
classification_notes: Described as semi-geometric and modular with exaggerated ink traps that create a visual splitting effect; the expressive, stylised construction places it firmly in the display category rather than a functional geometric sans.
summary: A semi-geometric modular display face with exaggerated ink traps that give characters the appearance of pulling apart at the joints.
description: Released in 2019, GTF Soya uses a modular construction with deliberately oversized ink traps to create the optical illusion that letterforms are slipping away from themselves. The family spans five weights and includes alternate numerals alongside broad European language support through 172 accent glyphs. The effect is best used at large sizes where the ink trap drama reads clearly.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Soya as a semi-geometric modular font featuring exaggerated ink traps, which give an illusion that the characters are slipping apart. It comes in five weights with alternate numerals and European language support, released in 2019.

name: Tekno
classification_notes: An extended display typeface with explicitly technological styling, designed for headline use at large sizes with a single weight and two styles (presumably regular and italic or alternate). Fits squarely in the display category with a techno/sci-fi subcategory.
summary: A single-weight extended display typeface with technological styling, released in 2018 with European language support across two styles.
description: Built for headline and display use, Tekno pairs an extended letterform with overtly technological detailing across one weight and two styles. The glyph set covers uppercase, lowercase, accented characters for European languages, numerals and a solid set of punctuation and symbols. A narrow brief executed with clarity, suited to titles, posters and branding where a digital or futuristic tone is needed.
foundry_blurb: Good Type Foundry describe Tekno as an extended display typeface with technologic features, available in one weight and two styles with European language support, released in 2018.
